title: 10 best herbal tinctures for sleep deprivation
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for sleep deprivation are concentrated liquid extracts of plants that are used as a natural remedy to improve sleep quality. They help with sleep deprivation by addressing the root causes such as stress, anxiety, and insomnia. Some examples of herbal tinctures that are commonly used for sleep deprivation include valerian root, passionflower, and chamomile. Valerian root is known for its sedative properties, passionflower helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, while chamomile has calming effects that can help induce sleep. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can improve their sleep patterns, reduce fatigue, and enhance their overall quality of life. For instance, a person who has been struggling with insomnia for months can find relief by taking a few drops of valerian root tincture before bedtime, leading to a more restful night's sleep and increased energy levels during the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains active compounds that influence the levels of certain neurotransmitters in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. These compounds, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, interact with gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) receptors, which play a crucial role in regulating the sleep-wake cycle. By enhancing GABA activity, valerian tincture can help alleviate sleep disturbances and improve overall sleep quality, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals struggling with sleep deprivation.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian tincture can help reduce the time it takes to fall asleep due to its sedative effects, primarily attributed to its active constituents, valerenic acid and valepotriates.

Anxiolytic properties: Valerian tincture can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which is crucial for individuals experiencing sleep deprivation, thanks to its active components like valerenic acid and isovaleric acid.

Improved sleep quality: Valerian tincture can enhance sleep quality by increasing the duration of deep, restorative sleep stages, partly due to its active constituents, including valerenic acid and linarin.

Antispasmodic properties: Valerian tincture can help relax muscles and alleviate tension, which can contribute to sleep disturbances, as a result of active constituents like valepotriates and isovaleric acid.

Absence of morning grogginess: Unlike many pharmaceutical sleep aids, valerian tincture is less likely to cause residual drowsiness or grogginess the following day, allowing users to feel refreshed and alert upon waking, due to its active ingredients' gentle and non-habit forming nature.
remedy_parts: Roots: The valerian root is the most commonly used part of the plant for making medicinal tea to treat sleep deprivation due to its high concentration of active compounds that promote relaxation and sleep.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are underground stems that are also used in tea preparations, as they contain similar sleep-promoting properties as the roots.
Flowers: Valerian flowers are occasionally included in tea blends for their calming effects, which can help alleviate anxiety and improve sleep quality.

Seeds: Valerian seeds contain essential oils that may contribute to the plant's sedative effects, although they are less commonly used in tea preparations due to their lower concentration of active compounds.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried valerian root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 5 ounces of vodka or rum over the valerian root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and take 1-2 dropperfuls before bed as needed for sleep deprivation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains compounds that have a sedative effect on the body. This herb, also known as hops, has been used for centuries in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. The active constituents in hops, such as bitter acids and volatile oils, are thought to increase the activity of a neurotransmitter called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain. GABA helps to regulate the communication between nerve cells and has a calming effect on the nervous system. By increasing GABA activity, humulus lupulus tincture can help to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Humulus lupulus tincture contains compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote sleep. The active constituents responsible for these sedative properties are mainly the bitter acids and essential oils, particularly myrcene, humulene, and farnesene.

Anxiolytic properties: The tincture can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common causes of sleep deprivation. The flavonoids and volatile oils in Humulus lupulus, such as lupulone and xanthohumol, contribute to its anxiolytic effects.

Hypnotic properties: Humulus lupulus tincture has been shown to have sleep-inducing qualities, which can be beneficial for individuals experiencing sleep deprivation. The sedative and anxiolytic properties of the tincture, combined with its hypnotic effects, make it an effective sleep aid.

Muscle relaxant properties: The tincture can help relieve muscle tension and spasms, which can improve overall sleep quality. The active constituents responsible for these muscle relaxant properties include the bitter acids and essential oils, particularly humulene and caryophyllene.

Antioxidant properties: Humulus lupulus tincture contains antioxidants that help protect cells from damage, which can contribute to overall health and well-being. The antioxidant properties of the tincture can also support the immune system, which can be weakened by sleep deprivation. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Humulus lupulus, such as xanthohumol and myrcenol, are responsible for its antioxidant effects.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers, also known as hops, contain relaxing properties that can help promote sleep.
Leaves: The leaves have calming effects that can help reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have sedative properties that can help alleviate sleep disorders.
Seeds: The seeds contain oils that can help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, aiding in sleep.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Humulus lupulus (hops) flowers into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the hops, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the spent hops and reserving the liquid.
Store the finished hops tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year. Recommended dosage is 1-2 dropperfuls (30-60 drops) before bedtime.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains active compounds, such as passionflower extract, that have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds help increase the levels of a neurotransmitter called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which promotes relaxation and sleep. By reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of calm, maypop tincture can help individuals who struggle with sleep deprivation by making it easier for them to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. Additionally, maypop tincture has been shown to improve the quality of sleep, leaving individuals feeling more refreshed and alert upon waking.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Maypop tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which is important for sleep deprivation as anxiety can exacerbate sleep problems; the active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids such as chloridine and isochlorogenic acid.

Sedative: Maypop tincture has sedative properties that help induce sleep, making it useful for sleep deprivation; the active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids such as rutin and isoquercitrin.

Hypnotic: Maypop tincture has hypnotic properties that promote the onset and maintenance of sleep, which is important for sleep deprivation; the active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils such as linalool and myrcene.

Muscle relaxant: Maypop tincture has muscle relaxant properties that help alleviate muscle tension and spasms, which can interfere with sleep; the active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids such as corydine and protopine.

Anticonvulsant: Maypop tincture has anticonvulsant properties that help reduce the likelihood of seizures, which can be a complication of sleep deprivation; the active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids such as corydine and protopine.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop tincture plant contain high levels of alkaloids, which can help promote relaxation and sleep.
Leaves: The leaves of the maypop tincture plant have calming properties and can help reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
Flowers: The flowers of the maypop tincture plant contain antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation, which can improve overall sleep quality.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop tincture plant are rich in essential fatty acids and can help regulate sleep patterns.
Bark: The bark of the maypop tincture plant contains active compounds that can help reduce stress and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 oz of dried maypop (Passiflora incarnata) flowers and leaves, and coarsely chop them.
Combine the chopped herbs with 1 pint of 80-proof vodka or rum in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herbs are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid from the herbs.
Store the finished maypop tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from light and heat; take 1-2 ml (30-60 drops) up to three times daily as needed for sleep support.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: California poppy tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains natural sedative properties that can help promote relaxation and calmness. This herb has been traditionally used for its sedative and analgesic effects, making it an effective natural remedy for insomnia and other sleep disorders. The tincture form of California poppy is easily absorbed by the body, allowing for quick and effective relief from sleep deprivation. Its soothing properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common causes of sleep disturbances. By promoting a sense of tranquility and restfulness, California poppy tincture can help individuals achieve a more restorative and rejuvenating sleep.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: California poppy tincture helps alleviate pain, which is important for sleep deprivation because unmanaged pain can disrupt sleep. The alkaloids in the plant, such as protopine and cryptopine, contribute to its pain-relieving effects.

Anxiolytic property: The tincture has calming and relaxing effects, reducing anxiety and promoting sleep. The isoquinoline alkaloids, including sanguinarine and chelerythrine, are believed to be responsible for these anxiolytic properties.

Sedative property: California poppy tincture helps induce sleep by acting as a mild sedative. This property is crucial for sleep deprivation as it can help users fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer. The alkaloids, including protopine, allocryptopine, and cryptopine, are believed to contribute to the sedative effects.

Hypnotic property: The tincture can help users achieve a deeper, more restorative sleep. This property is essential for sleep deprivation as it can help users recover from sleep loss and improve overall sleep quality. The isoquinoline alkaloids, such as sanguinarine and chelerythrine, are believed to be responsible for the hypnotic effects.

Muscle relaxant property: California poppy tincture can help relax muscles, reducing tension and promoting better sleep. This property is important for sleep deprivation because muscle tension and cramping can disrupt sleep. The alkaloids in the plant, including protopine and allocryptopine, contribute to its muscle relaxant effects.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of californidine, a compound that helps promote relaxation and sleep.
Leaves: Rich in alkaloids and antioxidants, which can help reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and fatty acids, which can help soothe the nervous system and improve sleep patterns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest California poppy flowers at peak bloom, choosing organic plants if possible.
Chop or grind 1 ounce of dried poppy flowers and add to a clean jar, filling it up to ¾ with 80-proof vodka.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, discarding the plant material and storing the liquid in a dark glass bottle.
Take 30 drops of the California poppy tincture 30 minutes before bedtime, adjusting the dosage as needed for sleep deprivation relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it promotes relaxation and calmness. This herb, with its soothing properties, helps reduce anxiety and stress, which are common causes of sleep disorders. Lemon balm contains a compound called rosmarinic acid, which increases the availability of GABA, a neurotransmitter that has a calming effect on the brain. Moreover, it has been found to improve the overall quality of sleep, making it an effective natural remedy for those suffering from sleep deprivation. By consuming lemon balm tincture before bedtime, individuals can enjoy a more restful and rejuvenating sleep.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic effect: Lemon balm tincture has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote calmness, which is important for sleep deprivation as it can help alleviate the stress and restlessness that often accompanies sleep disorders. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and citral.

Sedative effect: Lemon balm tincture has a sedative effect, which can help improve sleep quality and reduce sleep onset latency. The main active constituent responsible for this property is rosmarinic acid.

Improves GABAergic activity: Lemon balm tincture can enhance GABAergic activity in the brain, which can help promote relaxation and sleep. GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) is a neurotransmitter that inhibits nerve transmission in the central nervous system, producing a calming effect.

Antioxidant effect: Lemon balm tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which can contribute to sleep disorders. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and ferulic acid.

Neuroprotective effect: Lemon balm tincture has neuroprotective effects, which can help protect against neuronal damage and degeneration, both of which can contribute to sleep disorders. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and eugenol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid and citronellal, which have calming effects that may help improve sleep quality.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, contributing to the plant's soothing properties, aiding in sleep promotion.
Stems: Contain trace amounts of active compounds, which, when combined with leaves and flowers, enhance the tea's sleep-promoting benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh lemon balm leaves, chop finely, and place in a clean jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the chopped leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent herbs.
Store the finished tincture in a glass dropper bottle, taking 1-2 ml before bed as needed for sleep deprivation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. These compounds work by reducing anxiety and inducing a calming effect on the nervous system. When taken before bedtime, lavender tincture can help individuals fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer, leading to improved mood, cognitive function, and overall health. Additionally, lavender has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort that may be contributing to sleep disturbances. Overall, English lavender tincture is a natural and effective remedy for sleep deprivation, providing a safe and gentle alternative to prescription sleep aids.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: English lavender tincture helps reduce anxiety, which is important for sleep deprivation as anxiety can exacerbate sleep problems. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and linalyl acetate.
Sedative property: Lavender has a sedative effect, which aids in promoting sleep and improving sleep quality. This property is due to the presence of the terpene alcohols linalool and lavandulol.

Nervous system relaxant property: English lavender tincture helps relax the nervous system, which can alleviate tension and restlessness, common symptoms of sleep deprivation. The active constituents responsible for this property include apigenin and caryophyllene oxide.

Antidepressant property: Lavender has been shown to have antidepressant effects, which can be beneficial for sleep deprivation as depression often co-occurs with sleep disorders. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and limonene.

Neuroprotective property: English lavender tincture has neuroprotective properties, which can help protect the brain from damage caused by sleep deprivation. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in calming essential oils and adaptogens, promoting relaxation and sleep.
Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and mild sedative properties, aiding in reducing sleep disturbances.
Buds: Packed with soothing compounds, buds enhance the tea's effectiveness in alleviating sleep deprivation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh English lavender flowers, filling a clean, dry mason jar to 3/4 full.
Add 80-proof vodka until the flowers are completely submerged, covering by at least 1 inch.
Securely fasten a lid on the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers and transferring the tincture to a clean, labeled glass bottle.
Store the lavender tincture in a cool, dark place and take 1-2 dropperfuls (20-40 drops) before bedtime as needed for sleep deprivation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Catnip tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains nepetalactone, a compound that has a calming and relaxing effect on the body. This substance promotes relaxation by binding to receptors in the brain, which can help reduce anxiety and stress. Additionally, catnip has been shown to have sedative properties, making it an effective herbal remedy for insomnia and other sleep disorders. By promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, catnip tincture can help individuals achieve a more restful and restorative night's sleep, making it an ideal natural solution for those struggling with sleep deprivation.
remedy_properties: Sedative effect: Catnip tincture has a sedative effect that helps promote sleep and relaxation, making it useful for individuals experiencing sleep deprivation. This property is attributed to its active constituents, such as nepetalactone and valepotriates.

Anxiolytic effect: Catnip tincture can help reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, which is important for sleep deprivation as anxiety can exacerbate sleep problems. The anxiolytic effect is due to the presence of nepetalactone and essential oils in catnip.

Muscle relaxation: Catnip tincture has muscle-relaxing properties that can help alleviate tension and discomfort, allowing the body to relax and prepare for sleep. This is attributed to the presence of nepetalactone and its interaction with the GABA receptors in the brain.

Improved digestion: Catnip tincture can help improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion, which can interfere with sleep. This is due to the presence of iridoids and essential oils, which have been shown to have a positive effect on the digestive system.

Mild hypnotic effect: Catnip tincture has a mild hypnotic effect that can help individuals fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer, making it useful for managing sleep deprivation. This effect is attributed to the presence of valepotriates and nepetalactone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nepetalactone, a compound that induces relaxation and promotes sleep.
Buds: Contain high levels of essential oils, offering a potent source of calming properties for sleep deprivation.
Flowers: Packed with soothing agents that help reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality.
Stems: Offer a milder sedative effect, aiding in the relief of sleep disorders when combined with other parts.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried catnip herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding the plant material.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and dosage (1-2 mL up to three times daily) and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jujube tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains several compounds that promote relaxation and sleep. Jujube, also known as ziziphus spinosa, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat sleep disorders. The tincture is made from the fruit of the jujube tree and contains saponins, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which have sedative and hypnotic effects. These compounds help to regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote a deep, restful sleep. Additionally, jujube tincture is a natural remedy that is non-addictive and has few, if any, side effects, making it a safe and effective alternative to prescription sleep aids.
remedy_properties: Sedative effect: Jujube tincture has a calming and relaxing effect on the nervous system, which is important for sleep deprivation as it can help reduce anxiety and promote better sleep. This property is attributed to the active constituents, such as saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, that have sedative effects.

Hypnotic effect: Jujube tincture can help induce sleep and improve sleep quality by increasing the length of deep, restorative sleep stages. This is due to the presence of active compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have been shown to have hypnotic effects.

Anxiolytic effect: Jujube tincture can alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, which often contribute to sleep deprivation. This is because jujube contains active constituents like jujuboside A and B, which have anxiolytic properties that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

Neuroprotective effect: Jujube tincture has neuroprotective properties, which can help protect the brain from the damaging effects of sleep deprivation. This is due to the presence of antioxidant compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

Regulation of circadian rhythm: Jujube tincture can help regulate the body's circadian rhythm, which is essential for maintaining healthy sleep patterns. This is due to the presence of active constituents like melatonin and jujuboside A, which can help regulate the body's internal clock and promote healthy sleep-wake cycles.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Contain saponins and flavonoids that have sedative properties, making them useful for treating sleep deprivation.
Bark: Rich in alkaloids and triterpenoids that have calming effects, making it an effective ingredient for medicinal tea for insomnia.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have sedative and hypnotic effects, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Flowers: Have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, promoting better sleep.

Roots: Contain a variety of active compounds, including alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, that have sedative and hypnotic effects, making them a popular ingredient in traditional medicinal teas for sleep disorders.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried jujube dates and place them in a clean jar.
Add 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum to the jar, ensuring the jujubes are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
After the specified time, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, discarding the jujube solids. Take 1-2 mL before bedtime for sleep deprivation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common lime tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains certain compounds that have a calming and relaxing effect on the body. These compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Additionally, common lime tincture may help to regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, also known as the circadian rhythm. This can help to improve the quality of sleep and leave individuals feeling more refreshed and alert upon waking. Overall, common lime tincture can be a natural and effective way to combat sleep deprivation and improve sleep quality.
remedy_properties: Sedative effect: Lime tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and promote sleep. This is due to the presence of flavonoids such as hesperidin and naringenin.
Muscle relaxant: Lime tincture can help relax muscles, alleviating tension and discomfort that may disrupt sleep. This is attributed to the presence of alkaloids like limonin and nomilin.
Blood pressure regulation: Lime tincture may help regulate blood pressure, which can contribute to better sleep. This is due to the high vitamin C and flavonoid content.

Antioxidant properties: Lime tincture has strong antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from damage caused by stress and free radicals, promoting overall health and better sleep. This is thanks to the high concentration of vitamin C and flavonoids.

Immune system support: Lime tincture can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off infections and diseases that can disrupt sleep. This is due to the high vitamin C content and the presence of limonoids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and mildly sedative, helping to promote sleep.
Leaves: Contain soothing compounds that can help reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality.
Buds: Packed with essential oils, providing a strong calming effect to aid sleep.
Stems: Contain mild sedative properties, helping to alleviate sleep deprivation symptoms.
Seeds: Source of sleep-promoting compounds, aiding in falling and staying asleep.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of fresh lime leaves and stems, and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of vodka and 1 cup of distilled water to the jar, ensuring the herbs are fully submerged.
Securely close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the steeping period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean glass dropper bottle, and store it in a cool, dark place; take 1-2 dropperfuls before bedtime as needed for sleep deprivation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo tincture helps with sleep deprivation because it contains alkaloids that have a calming effect on the central nervous system. These alkaloids, such as tetrahydropalmatine, can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Additionally, yanhusuo has been shown to increase the duration of stage 4 sleep, which is the deep, restorative stage of sleep that is essential for feeling refreshed and rejuvenated in the morning. By improving the quality and duration of sleep, yanhusuo tincture can help to alleviate the negative effects of sleep deprivation, such as fatigue, irritability, and difficulty concentrating.
remedy_properties: Sedative property: Yanhusuo tincture has a sedative effect that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is important for sleep deprivation because it can help individuals fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer. This property is attributed to the alkaloids tetrahydropalmatine (THP) and corydine.

Analgesic property: Yanhusuo tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of well-being, which is important for sleep deprivation because pain and discomfort can interfere with sleep quality. The alkaloids THP and tetrahydrocoptisine (THC) are responsible for this property.

Hypnotic property: Yanhusuo tincture has hypnotic effects that can help induce sleep and deepen sleep, which is important for sleep deprivation because it can help individuals achieve a more restorative sleep. The alkaloids THP and stemmadenine contribute to this property.

Antispasmodic property: Yanhusuo tincture has antispasmodic effects that can help relieve muscle tension and spasms, which is important for sleep deprivation because muscle tension can cause discomfort and interfere with sleep. The alkaloids THP and tertiary fangchinoline are responsible for this property.

Cognitive-enhancing property: Yanhusuo tincture has cognitive-enhancing effects that can help improve memory and cognitive function, which is important for sleep deprivation because sleep deprivation can impair cognitive function and memory. The alkaloids THP and fangchinoline contribute to this property.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yanhusuo's roots contain alkaloids that help regulate the sleep-wake cycle.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of yanhusuo have sedative properties that can help improve sleep quality.
Flowers: Yanhusuo's flowers contain antioxidants that can help reduce stress and promote better sleep.
Seeds: The seeds of yanhusuo have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, aiding in sleep.
Buds: Yanhusuo's buds are rich in essential oils that can help soothe the mind and induce sleep.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Yanhusuo root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or pure grain alcohol over the chopped Yanhusuo root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid from the Yanhusuo root.
Label the bottle with the name, dosage, and date, and store in a cool, dark place. Recommended dosage is 1-2 mL, taken 30 minutes before bedtime.
